name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Tracker Hub
description: The `esp32_ble_tracker` component creates a global hub so that you can track bluetooth low energy devices using your ESP32 node.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for this ESP32 BLE Hub.

config_entries.label: Max Connections
config_entries.description: **DEPRECATED** - This option has been moved to the [Esp32 Ble](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble/) component. Please configure `max_connections` there instead. This option is kept for backward compatibility only. This option will be removed in ESPHome 2026.10.0.

config_entries.label: Scan Parameters
config_entries.description: Advanced parameters for configuring the scan behavior of the ESP32. See also [this guide by Texas Instruments](https://dev.ti.com/tirex/explore/content/simplelink_academy_cc2640r2sdk_5_10_02_00/modules/blestack/ble_scan_adv_basic/ble_scan_adv_basic.html#scanning-basics) for reference.

config_entries.label: Active
config_entries.description: Whether to actively send scan requests to request more data after having received an advertising packet. With some devices this is necessary to receive all data, but also drains those devices' power a bit more. Some devices don't need this, in that case you can save power and RF pollution by setting it to `false`. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Continuous
config_entries.description: Whether to scan continuously (forever) or to only scan when asked to start a scan (with start_scan action). Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Duration
config_entries.description: The duration of each complete scan. This has no real impact on the device but can be used to debug the BLE stack. Defaults to `5min`.

config_entries.label: Interval
config_entries.description: The interval between each consecutive scan window. This is the time the ESP spends on each of the 3 BLE advertising channels. Defaults to `320ms`.

config_entries.label: Window
config_entries.description: The time the ESP is actively listening for packets on a channel during each scan interval. If this is close to the `interval` value, the ESP will spend more time listening to packets (but also consume more power). Defaults to `30ms`

config_entries.label: Software Coexistence
config_entries.description: When enabled, software coexistence will briefly prioritize Bluetooth over Wi-Fi during the initial establishment of BLE connections, which can improve reliability. Only available if `wifi` component is configured. Defaults to `true`.
